cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about a search group formed by some American scientists. I need to figure out which answer choice is correct based on the passage. First, I'll recall the details from the text. The passage mentions that in 1975, some American scientists formed a search group. They used an underwater camera that took pictures every seventy seconds. Some of the pictures seemed to show a red-brown creature with a four-meter body and a long neck. Because of this, many people began to believe in the monster. However, the passage also states that even today we can't be certain. Now, looking at the answer choices: A) found the monster itself – The text doesn't say they found the actual monster. It says the pictures seemed to show a monster, but there's no confirmation that it was the real thing. So A is probably not correct. B) found a huge cave under water – The passage mentions that underwater TV cameras found a huge underwater cave in a previous effort (before 1975, during the 1961-1971 period). The 1975 search group used a camera but didn't find a cave, so B is not the answer. C) believed that there wasn't any monster at all – The text says that after the 1975 pictures, many people began to believe in the monster.
